content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 65: The spreadsheet explainer

The free 14 days got the reps moving. The paid track is where the habit has to become professionally useful. Today belongs to analysis and business case: one practical workout, one usable output, one more piece of your AI working system.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
---------------
ANALYSIS & SYNTHESIS | STEADY STATE | 20 min

THE BRIEF:
Use AI to explain a table without pretending it has done the maths unless you provide it.

THE SITUATION:
Bring a small table or summary numbers you can share. Ask for interpretation.

PROMPT SCAFFOLD:
Do not copy this blindly. Adapt it to your work.

"I am working on [real situation]. My goal is [specific outcome]. The audience or user is [person/team/customer/reader]. Here is the source material or context I am allowed to share: [paste safe material]. The constraints are [length, tone, deadline, format, privacy, things to avoid]. Help me with: Use AI to explain a table without pretending it has done the maths unless you provide it. Before producing the final output, tell me what assumptions you are making and what information would improve the result."

YOUR REPS:
1. Provide the table and ask the model to explain patterns, anomalies, and questions.

2. Ask it to distinguish observed facts from possible explanations.

3. Write the caveat you would include if sharing the interpretation.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
- Facts and explanations are separated.
- Anomalies become questions.
- The caveat is visible.

TRANSFER:
This transfers to metric reviews, business cases, scenario planning, budget arguments, assumption checks, and places where numbers need judgement rather than theatrical precision.

COACHING NOTE:
Models are good at language around data. They are not a substitute for checking the data pipeline, definitions, or arithmetic.

BEHAVIOURAL DESIGN:
Keep the caveat with the output. This is your implementation intention for today: after you finish the workout, decide exactly where this output will live and when you will use it again.

MINIMUM VIABLE REP:
If today is chaos, do the smallest honest version: spend five minutes creating the input and five minutes reviewing the output. Do not aim for a masterpiece. Aim for a usable artefact and one clear improvement. Keeping the chain alive matters because skill comes from repeated contact with real work, not from admiring the concept from a safe distance.

---------------
Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive. Treat AI outputs as drafts, not authority. Verify factual claims before using them in public, financial, medical, legal, HR, or high-stakes contexts.
The AI Habit: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
